---
title: "Session 10 teaching notes: Python 7"
session_id: session-10
event_id: null
---

## Session 10 — Loop control, functions, and dictionaries

p.116: nr 2 homework.

additional exercise (Insurance) - PR shares it

Functions
- mind. eine Klausuraufgabe

p. 121/122
Order, in-session: 4, 5, 1, 6 (if time permits)
Homework: 2-3

Dictionaries: less relevant for the exam (do not explicitly announce as not relevant at the beginning) - maybe as multiple-choice.


## Exercises

| Page(s) | Exercise                     | Summary                                                                                                                                        | Files                                                                                                        | In-class / take-home |
| ------: | ---------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------- |
|     116 | Loop termination             | Stop an otherwise infinite loop when zero is entered and add up to three attempts to the dice game.                                            | `StopLoop.py`<br>`Dicegame3.py`                                                                              |                      |
| 121–122 | Functions                    | Create functions for digit counting, list multiplication, Harshad numbers, factorials, combinations, and a multistage slot-machine simulation. | `CountDigits.py`<br>`Listmult.py`<br>`Harshad.py`<br>`Factorial.py`<br>`Combinations.py`<br>`SlotMachine.py` |                      |
|     133 | Dictionaries and persistence | Maintain a nested dictionary of friends, modify its contents, and save and reload it from a file.                                              | `Friends.py`<br>`friends.db`                                                                                 |                      |

::: {.teaching-break}
☕ Break — 10 minutes
:::

## Summary and announcements (next session)

TODO


<!--
    ## Session overview

    - **Focus:** Python
    - **Session title:** Python 1
    - **Coverage target:** Until **Accessing data**

    ## Before class

    - [ ] Review the session slides/materials.
    - [ ] Prepare files, datasets, and examples.
    - [ ] Note any announcements or reminders.

    ## Timing record

    Use this table while preparing or after teaching to record the actual timing.

    | Planned time | Actual time | Topic/activity | Notes for next time |
    |---|---|---|---|
    | 0--10 min |  | Setup / recap |  |
    | 10--30 min |  | Main input |  |
    | 30--60 min |  | Guided practice |  |
    | 60--80 min |  | Independent practice / questions |  |
    | 80--90 min |  | Wrap-up |  |

    ## Notes during class

    - TBD

    ## Follow-up

    - [ ] Update examples or instructions.
    - [ ] Record common questions.
    - [ ] Add reminders for the next session.
-->
